data.title: Valero Energy Corp. reports $1.1 billion net income in Q4 2025 vs $281 million in Q4 2024, $3.73 per share vs $0.88.
data.description: In San Antonio, Valero Energy Corporation (NYSE: VLO, “Valero”) announced a significant increase in net income attributable to Valero stockholders for the fourth quarter of 2025. The company reported a net income of $1.1 billion, or $3.73 per share, compared to $281 million, or $0.88 per share, in the same quarter of 2024. Adjusted for certain items, the adjusted net income attributable to Valero stockholders was $1.2 billion, or $3.82 per share, for the fourth quarter of 2025. This represents a notable growth in earnings from the previous year.

data.title: Ares provides $1.6B in debt financing for Suave Brands and Elida Beauty merger, creating Evermark.
data.description: Ares Management Corporation (NYSE: ARES) disclosed today that its Ares Credit funds acted as the administrative agent for $1.6 billion in debt financing to facilitate the merger of Suave Brands Company and Elida Beauty, both portfolio companies of Yellow Wood Partners. The merger resulted in the formation of Evermark, LLC, a new entity in the industry. Evermark emerges as a prominent global platform following the integration, aiming to become a frontrunner in its sector.

data.title: Report on Brazil Defense Industry projects market size & trends from 2025-2030 with forecasts, budget allocation, regulations & competitive landscape.
data.description: A report titled "The Brazil Defense Market - Size and Trends, Budget Allocation, Regulations, Key Acquisitions, Competitive Landscape and Forecast, 2025-2030" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. This report presents a forecast of the market size and growth rate for the next five years. It includes an industry analysis highlighting market drivers, emerging technology trends, and challenges encountered by market players. The report provides valuable insights into the Brazil defense market.

data.title: OSL Group secures $200M for stablecoin and payments expansion, eyes acquisitions and global growth.
data.description: OSL Group, a company based in Hong Kong that is publicly listed, is planning to acquire new businesses and expand globally. This decision comes after they successfully raised additional capital. The focus of their expansion will be on scaling up their stablecoin and digital payments operations. This move is part of their strategy to grow and solidify their presence in the market.

data.title: Solana validators decline by 68% due to high costs, forcing smaller operators offline amid zero-fee competition.
data.description: The number of validators on the Solana network has decreased by 68% since 2023 due to increasing expenses and competition from zero-fee alternatives, leading smaller node operators to go offline.
